title: The Reason for Itakh 's Pilgrimage This Year
description: Itikh was reportedly a Khazar slave (ghulam), a cook, belonging to Sallam al-Abrash. Al-Mu'tasim bought Itakh from Sallam in r99 (814-1 5).281 As Itakh was manly and intrepid, al-Mu'tasim, and al- Wathiq after him, promoted him to the position where many of the administrative functions (a'mdl) of the central government were assigned to him. Al-Mu'tasim appointed him governor of the security police (ma'unah) of Samarra along with Ishaq b. Ibrahim. Both Itakh and Ishaq b. Ibrahim were represented by deputies.

sources.citation: al-Tabari, The History of al-Tabari (SUNY), vol. 34
